title: Regulated expression of card11-pik3r3 fusion protein
abstract: The present disclosure relates generally to compositions and methods for improving T cell therapy.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A recombinant cell comprising : a) a first nucleic acid encoding a chimeric receptor, the chimeric receptor comprising, from N-terminus to C-terminus: (i) an extracellular ligand-binding domain having a binding affinity for a selected ligand; (ii) a linking sequence; (iii) a transmembrane domain (TMD) comprising one or more ligandinducible proteolytic cleavage sites; and (iv) an intracellular domain (ICD) comprising, in any order: (A) an intracellular signaling domain (SD) comprising at least one costimulatory domain derived from a signaling molecule, and an activation domain, and (B) a transcriptional regulator, wherein binding of the selected ligand to the extracellular ligand-binding domain induces cleavage at the one or more ligand-inducible proteolytic cleavage sites disposed between the ICD and the linking sequence, wherein the chimeric receptor does not comprise a LIN-12-Notch repeat (LNR) and/or a heterodimerization domain (HD) of a Notch receptor; and b) a second nucleic acid encoding a caspase recruitment domain family member 11 (CARD11) polypeptide comprising (i) a caspase recruitment domain (CARD) and (ii) one or more mutations, wherein the second nucleic acid is operably linked to a promoter and a response element that is capable of binding to the transcriptional regulator; wherein expression of the CARD11 polypeptide is modulated by the transcriptional regulator. 2. A recombinant cell comprising: a) a first nucleic acid encoding a first polypeptide of a multi-chain chimeric receptor, wherein the first polypeptide comprises, from N-terminus to C-terminus: 

 (i) an extracellular ligand-binding domain having a binding affinity for a selected ligand; (ii) a linking sequence; (iii) a first transmembrane domain (TMD) comprising a first modified interface and one or more ligand-inducible proteolytic cleavage sites; (iv) a first intracellular domain (ICD) comprising a transcriptional regulator, wherein binding of the selected ligand to the extracellular ligand-binding domain induces cleavage at the one or more ligand-inducible proteolytic cleavage sites; b) a third nucleic acid encoding a second polypeptide of the multi-chain chimeric receptor, wherein the second polypeptide comprises, from N-terminus to C-terminus: (i) a second TMD comprising a second modified interface; and (ii) a second ICD comprising (A) at least one costimulatory domain derived from a signaling molecule, and (B) an activation domain, wherein the first and the second modified interfaces each comprises an amino acid residue having a charged side chain, wherein the amino acid residues in the first and the second modified interfaces have opposite charges, wherein the first polypeptide is coupled to the second polypeptide via the first modified interface and the second modified interface; wherein the multi-chain chimeric receptor does not comprise a LIN-12-Notch repeat (LNR) and/or a heterodimerization domain (HD) of a Notch receptor; and c) a second nucleic acid encoding a caspase recruitment domain family member 11 (CARD11) polypeptide comprising (i) a caspase recruitment domain (CARD) and (ii) one or more mutations, wherein the second nucleic acid is operably linked to a promoter and a response element that is capable of binding to the transcriptional regulator; wherein expression of the CARD11 polypeptide is modulated by the transcriptional regulator. 3. The recombinant cell of claim 1 or 2, wherein binding of the selected ligand to the extracellular ligand-binding domain induces proximal signaling cascades through the at least one costimulatory domain and/or the activation domain. 4. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 1-3, wherein binding of the selected ligand to the extracellular ligand-binding domain releases the transcriptional regulator from the chimeric polypeptide. 5. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the response element is comprised within the promoter. 6. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the extracellular domain comprises an antigen-binding moiety capable of binding to the selected ligand on the surface of a second cell. 7. The recombinant cell of claim 6, wherein the second cell is a pathogenic cell. 8. The recombinant cell of claim 7, wherein the second cell is a human cell. 9. The recombinant cell of claim 8, wherein the human cell is a tumor cell. 10. The recombinant cell of claim 9, wherein the human cell is a terminally differentiated cell. 11. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the ligand comprises a protein or a carbohydrate. 12. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the ligand is selected from the group consisting of CD1, CD1a, CD1b, CD1c, CD1d, CD1e, CD2, CD3Î´, CD3Î¶, CD3Î³, CD3Îµ, CD4, CD5, CD7, CD8a, CD8b, CD19, CD20, CD21, CD22, CD23, CD24, CD25, CD27, CD28, CD30, CD33, CD34, CD38, CD40, CD44v6, CD45, CD46, CD47 CD48, CD52, CD59, CD66, CD70, CD71, CD72, CD73, CD79A, CD79B, CD80 (B7.1), CD86 (B7.2), CD94, CD95, CD97, CD123, CD134, CD140 (PDGFR4), CD152, CD154, CD158, CD171, CD178, CD179, CD179a, CD181 (CXCR1), CD182 (CXCR2), CD183 (CXCR3), CD210, CD213A2, CD246, CD252, CD253, CD261, CD262, CD272, CD273 (PD-L2), CD274 (PD-L1), CD276 (B7H3), CD279, CD295, CD339 (JAG1), CD340 (HER2), CDH17, CEA, CLECL1, CLL-1,

The recombinant cell of any one of claims 6-17, wherein the antigen-binding moiety specifically binds to a tumor-associated antigen selected from the group consisting of ADGRE2, CD19, B7H3 (CD276), BCMA (CD269), ALPPL2, CD123, CD171, CD179a, CD20, CD213A2, CD22, CD24, CD246, CD272, CD30, CD33, CD38, CD44v6, CD46, CD71, CD97, CEA, CLDN6, Claudin 18.2, CLECL1, CS-1, DLL3, EGFR, EGFRvIII, EGFR806, ELF2M, EpCAM, EphA2, Ephrin B2, FAP, FLT3, GD2, GD3, GM3, GPRC5D, HER2 (ERBB2/neu), HER2p95, IGLL1, IL-11RÎ±, KIT (CD117), KLK2, KLK3, LY6G6D, MUC1, MUC16, NCAM, PAP, PDGFR-Î², PRSS21, PSCA, PSMA, ROR1, SIRPÎ±, SSEA-4, TAG72, TEM1/CD248, 

TEM7R, TSHR, VEGFR2, ALPI, citrullinated vimentin, cMet, GUCY2C, STEAP1, STEAP2, and Axl. 21. The recombinant cell of claim 20, wherein the tumor-associated antigen is CD19, BCMA, CEA, HER2, MUC1, CD20, ALPPL2, SIRPÎ±, or EGFR. 22. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 16-19, wherein the antigen-binding moiety specifically binds to a tumor-associated antigen selected from the group consisting of CD1, CD1a, CD1b, CD1c, CD1d, CD1e, CD2, CD3Î´, CD3Î¶, CD3Î³, CD3Îµ, CD4, CD5, CD7, CD8a, CD8b, CD19, CD20, CD21, CD22, CD23, CD24, CD25, CD27, CD28, CD30, CD33, CD34, CD38, CD40, CD44v6, CD45, CD46, CD47 CD48, CD52, CD59, CD66, CD70, CD71, CD72, CD73, CD79A, CD79B, CD80 (B7.1), CD86 (B7.2), CD94, CD95, CD97, CD123, CD134, CD140 (PDGFR4), CD152, CD154, CD158, CD171, CD178, CD179, CD179a, CD181 (CXCR1), CD182 (CXCR2), CD183 (CXCR3), CD210, CD213A2, CD246, CD252, CD253, CD261, CD262, CD272, CD273 (PD-L2), CD274 (PD-L1), CD276 (B7H3), CD279, CD295, CD339 (JAG1), CD340 (HER2), CDH17, CEA, CLECL1, CLL-1, CLDN6, CLDN18.2, CS1, DLL3, LY6G6D, GCC, PRAME, EGFR, EGFRvIII, FGFR2, AFP, CA125, MUC-1, MAGE, ALPI, alkaline phosphatase placental-like 2 (ALPPL2), B-cell maturation antigen (BCMA), green fluorescent protein (GFP), enhanced green fluorescent protein (eGFP), KLK2, KLK3, Mesothelin, IL13Ra2, signal regulatory protein Î± (SIRPÎ±), TCRalpha, TCRbeta, TSHR, GD2, GD3, Tn Ag, cMET, Axl, ROR1, ROR2, GPC1, GPC2, GPC3, FLT3, TAG72, CEA, EPCAM, KIT (CD117), IL-13Ra2, IL-11Ra, PSCA, PRSS21, VEGFR2, LewisY, PDGFRÎ², SSEA-4, folate receptor alpha, ERBB2 (Her2/neu), MUC1, MUC16, NCAM, prostase, PAP, ELF2M, Ephrin B2, IGF-I receptor, CAIX, LMP2, gplOO, bcr-abl, tyrosinase, EphA2, STEAP1, STEAP2, fucosyl GM1, sLe, GM3, TGS5, HMWMAA, o-acetyl-GD2, folate receptor beta, TEM1/CD248, TEM7R, ROPN1, GPRC5D, CXORF61, ALK, Polysialic acid, PLAC1, GloboH, NY-BR-1, UPK2, HAVCR1, ADRB3, PANX3, GPR20, LY6K, OR51E2, TARP, WT1, NY-ESO-1, LAGE-la, MAGE-Al, legumain, HPV E6,E7, MAGE Al, ETV6-AML,

The recombinant cell of claim 27, wherein the hinge domain is capable of promoting oligomer formation of the chimeric polypeptide via intermolecular disulfide bonding. 29. The recombinant cell of claim 27, wherein the hinge domain is derived from a CD8Î± hinge domain, a CD28 hinge domain, a CDI 52 hinge domain, a PD-1 hinge domain, a CTLA4 hinge domain, an OX40 hinge domain, an IgGl hinge domain, an IgG2 hinge domain, an IgG3 hinge domain, and an IgG4 hinge domain, or a functional variant of any thereof. 30. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the linking sequence is derived from a CD28 hinge domain or a functional variant thereof. 34. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the linking sequence comprises an amino acid sequence having at least 80% sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 8. 35. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the TMD comprises a polypeptide sequence having at least 80%, at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 95%, or 100%, sequence identity to SEQ ID NO: 5. 38. The recombinant cell of claim 37, wherein the TMD comprises an amino acid substitution at amino acid position 18 and/or 19 of SEQ ID NO: 5. 39. The recombinant cell of claim 38, wherein the TMD comprises an amino acid substitution corresponding to G18A and/or V19A of SEQ ID NO: 5. 40. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the TMD comprises a polypeptide sequence having at least 80%, at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 95%, or 100%, sequence identity to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 94-195. 41. The recombinant cell of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the TMD comprises a polypeptide sequence having at least 80%, at least 85%, at least 90%, at least 95%, or 100%, sequence identity to any one of SEQ ID NOS: 94-95, 99, 113, 132, 136, 147, and 157. 42. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 2-41, wherein the first and the second polypeptides of the multi-chain chimeric receptor are not covalently connected. 43. The recombinant cell of any one of claims 2-42, wherein the first modified interface comprises a positively charged residue, and wherein the second modified interface comprises a negatively charged residue, and wherein the first and the second polypeptides of the multi-chain chimeric receptor are coupled via the electrostatic force between the first and the second modified interfaces. 44.
